pages.text: Alchemy is as powerful as it is broad a category. First and foremost, I should procure some bottles to store the various liquids I will need in an easy to carry and access way. Moreover, I can turn normal bottles into $(item)$(t:Crafting a glass bottle alone turns it into a liquid container of 250mb; combining two allows to carry 500mb, half a bucket.)measuring bottles$() to store various liquids until I need to process them.
Most concoctions that do not use their own ingredients or water as their base will instead require another liquid.
pages.b_t: Wood alcohol is easy to distill from sawdust in a kettle
pages.c_t: Lunar water is an important resource with many uses. Mainly obtained with a night-jar
pages.d_t: Reasonably rare, it can appear in alps, shields, and similar alpine biomes
pages.text2: I might also be able to locate some of these in abandoned laboratories, so I better keep an eye out. Having early access to some bases could suppose a significant advantage.

pages.text: After gaining access to the nether, I have identified 'blood' and unstable spirit fire as viable bases.

$(#a84b0f)I will progress my research once I've secured some bottles.$() THIS PAGE SHOULD ONLY APPEAR BEFORE THE PLAYER OBTAINS A BOTTLE, using triumph:advancements/techtree/bottle. ANY OTHER BEHAVIOR IS WRONG.
